// GeneratedAsIdentitySequenceOption is used to populate the sequence options for the column information schema.
// An empty string will populate default values and null will generate null values.
// Override GeneratedAsIdentitySequenceOption default values with specified SeqOptions.
@@ -4466,6 +4466,155 @@ SELECT * FROM t_add_generated ORDER BY a
+# PG18: ADD GENERATED ... AS IDENTITY (SEQUENCE NAME ...) lets users name the
+# backing sequence explicitly instead of using the auto-generated
+# <table>_<column>_seq pattern. The ALTER TABLE form works under both the
+CREATE TABLE t_seqname (a INT NOT NULL, b INT NOT NULL, c INT NOT NULL, d INT NOT NULL, FAMILY (a,b,c,d))
+ALTER TABLE t_seqname ALTER COLUMN a ADD GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY (SEQUENCE NAME my_seq_a)
+# The sequence is created with the user-supplied name and is owned by the column.
+SELECT relname FROM pg_class WHERE relname = 'my_seq_a' AND relkind = 'S'
+# Mixing SEQUENCE NAME with other sequence options.
+ALTER TABLE t_seqname ALTER COLUMN b ADD GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY (START WITH 100 INCREMENT BY 5 SEQUENCE NAME my_seq_b)
+# SEQUENCE NAME is not persisted into the column's stored sequence options.
+# SHOW CREATE TABLE shows the start/increment but never SEQUENCE NAME.
+  a INT8 NOT NULL GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY,
+  b INT8 NOT NULL GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY (START 100 INCREMENT 5),
+  rowid INT8 NOT VISIBLE NOT NULL DEFAULT unique_rowid(),
+  FAMILY fam_0_a_b_c_d_rowid (a, b, c, d, rowid)
+# Schema-qualified explicit names work and the sequence ends up in the named schema.
+ALTER TABLE t_seqname ALTER COLUMN c ADD GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY (SEQUENCE NAME s_seqname.my_seq_c)
+FROM pg_class c JOIN pg_namespace n ON c.relnamespace = n.oid
+# A name collision with an existing relation is a hard error (PG semantics —
+# no auto-suffix disambiguation for explicitly named sequences).
+ALTER TABLE t_seqname ALTER COLUMN d ADD GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY (SEQUENCE NAME my_seq_d_taken)
+# CREATE TABLE form with SEQUENCE NAME runs through the legacy CREATE TABLE
+# code path under both schema changers.
+CREATE TABLE t_seqname_create (id INT GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY (SEQUENCE NAME my_create_seq))
+# Sanity: dropping the identity-owned sequence directly without CASCADE is
+# rejected; DROP TABLE is the supported way to clean up both the table and its
+statement error pq: cannot drop sequence my_create_seq because other objects depend on it
+# DROP SEQUENCE CASCADE on an identity-owned sequence: CRDB cascades through
+# and drops the sequence (note: this differs from PG, which rejects CASCADE
+# on a sequence that backs an identity column with the same "column requires
+# it" error). This is pre-existing behavior shared with auto-named identity
+# sequences and is unrelated to the SEQUENCE NAME clause.
+CREATE TABLE t_seqname_cascade (id INT GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY (SEQUENCE NAME my_cascade_seq))
+# SEQUENCE NAME is not allowed in CREATE SEQUENCE / ALTER SEQUENCE.
+statement error pq: SEQUENCE NAME is only valid for identity columns
+statement error pq: SEQUENCE NAME is only valid for identity columns
a int GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY (START WITH 2 INCREMENT 3),
+                                 // SEQUENCE NAME is only valid as a GENERATED AS IDENTITY option;

// Create the sequence and fetch the element after. The full descriptor
// will be created after the build phase, so we cannot fully resolve it.
+// qualifyExplicitSeqName resolves any unqualified parts of an explicit
+// SEQUENCE NAME against the owning table's database/schema, and panics with a
+// duplicate-relation error if an object with that name already exists. This
+// matches PostgreSQL's behavior, which does not auto-suffix user-supplied
func getNextAvailableSeqName(b BuildCtx, colName tree.Name, tn *tree.TableName) *tree.TableName {
// Start with a fixed sequence number and find the first one
+	// The PG18 SEQUENCE NAME clause introduces a relation name into the AST that
+	// the annotator would otherwise reject as unresolved. The sequence does not
+	// exist yet (we are about to create it), so mark the name as non-existent so
+	// the annotator skips qualification checks for it.
// Step 2. create a sequence and default expression
colDef, expr := alterTableCreateColumnSequence(b, colDef, tn, serialNormalizationMode, colTypeElem.Type)
ctx.WriteString(" GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY")
// TODO(janexing): remove the leading and ending space of the
// SequenceOptions represents a list of sequence options.
+// ExtractIdentitySeqName returns the explicit sequence name supplied via the
+// PG18 SEQUENCE NAME clause (if any) and a copy of the option list with that
+// entry removed. The SEQUENCE NAME entry is metadata about how to name the
+// sequence and never makes it into the persisted sequence options.
+	// NameVal carries the explicit sequence name supplied by the
+	// PG18 SEQUENCE NAME clause when this option appears on a
+	// GENERATED AS IDENTITY column. It is meaningless in other
// Names of options on CREATE SEQUENCE.
+	// SeqOptName is the PG18 "SEQUENCE NAME <name>" option for
+	// identity columns. It is only valid inside the parenthesized
+	// option list of GENERATED { ALWAYS | BY DEFAULT } AS IDENTITY.
generatedConstraint = pretty.Keyword("GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY")
